sections.question: I am writing to you regarding a conflict of interest question prior to my filing for the office of the Mayor in Chula Vista, California. My husband and I own a substantial stock interest in Ultronics, Inc., the managing general partner of Chula Vista cable; a cable television franchise holder for the city of Chula Vista, California. Under the terms of the City Charter (portion enclosed), any stock ownership over 3% in the cable company would apparently negate my ability to hold the Mayor's office, if I were to be elected. Because California is a Community property State, it appears that it would be difficult for me to reduce my holdings to less than 3% while retaining my community property interests. I seriously wish to run for the office but I need some type of written opinion from your office before I undergo the trials and tribulations of running for a public office.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the requester hold the office of Mayor in Chula Vista while she and her husband own substantial stock (over 3%) in Ultronics, Inc., the managing general partner of the city's cable television franchise, given California's community property laws?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document includes the requester's full letter and relevant portions of the Chula Vista City Charter (Sections 505-510) regarding conflicts of interest and financial interests. No FPPC response or conclusion is present in this document. The charter excerpt includes Section 508 which addresses financial interests in contracts.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: I am writing to you regarding a conflict of interest question prior to my filing for the office of the Mayor in Chula Vista, California. My husband and I own a substantial stock interest in Ultronics, Inc., the managing general partner of Chula Vista cable; a cable television franchise holder for the city of Chula Vista, California. Under the terms of the City Charter (portion enclosed), any stock ownership over 3% in the cable company would apparently negate my ability to hold the Mayor's office, if I were to be elected. Because California is a Community property State, it appears that it would be difficult for me to reduce my holdings to less than 3% while retaining my community property interests. I seriously wish to run for the office but I need some type of written opinion from your office before I undergo the trials and tribulations of running for a public office.

embedding.summary: Barbara Altbaun requests an FPPC opinion regarding whether she can run for Mayor of Chula Vista while she and her husband own stock exceeding 3% in Ultronics, Inc., which manages the city's cable television franchise. She seeks clarification before filing for office due to potential conflicts under the city charter.
